turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="#ffffff"/>

  <!-- Ground / foundation -->
  <rect x="86" y="360" width="340" height="40" rx="6" fill="#d9d9de" stroke="#b7b7c1" stroke-width="3"/>

  <!-- Brick stack (suggests building) -->
  <g transform="translate(110,290)">
    <!-- bottom row -->
    <g>
      <rect x="0" y="70" width="292" height="26" fill="#c65a3a" stroke="#a44a31" stroke-width="2"/>
      <rect x="13" y="83" width="266" height="0" fill="none"/>
      <rect x="0" y="70" width="292" height="26" fill="none"/>
    </g>

    <!-- bricks with staggered pattern -->
    <g>
      <!-- row 1 -->
      <rect x="0" y="42" width="292" height="28" fill="#c85d3f" stroke="#a44a31" stroke-width="2"/>
      <g stroke="#a44a31" stroke-width="2" opacity="0.95">
        <!-- vertical joints -->
        <line x1="36" y1="42" x2="36" y2="70"/>
        <line x1="72" y1="42" x2="72" y2="70"/>
        <line x1="108" y1="42" x2="108" y2="70"/>
        <line x1="144" y1="42" x2="144" y2="70"/>
        <line x1="180" y1="42" x2="180" y2="70"/>
        <line x1="216" y1="42" x2="216" y2="70"/>
        <line x1="252" y1="42" x2="252" y2="70"/>
        <!-- horizontal joint line accent -->
        <line x1="0" y1="56" x2="292" y2="56"/>
      </g>
    </g>

    <g>
      <!-- row 2 (staggered) -->
      <rect x="18" y="14" width="274" height="28" fill="#c65a3a" stroke="#a44a31" stroke-width="2"/>
      <g stroke="#a44a31" stroke-width="2" opacity="0.95">
        <line x1="54" y1="14" x2="54" y2="42"/>
        <line x1="90" y1="14" x2="90" y2="42"/>
        <line x1="126" y1="14" x2="126" y2="42"/>
        <line x1="162" y1="14" x2="162" y2="42"/>
        <line x1="198" y1="14" x2="198" y2="42"/>
        <line x1="234" y1="14" x2="234" y2="42"/>
        <line x1="270" y1="14" x2="270" y2="42"/>
        <line x1="18" y1="28" x2="292" y2="28"/>
      </g>
    </g>

    <!-- small top cap bricks -->
    <rect x="56" y="0" width="180" height="22" fill="#b94f33" stroke="#943a28" stroke-width="2"/>
    <g stroke="#943a28" stroke-width="2" opacity="0.9">
      <line x1="92" y1="0" x2="92" y2="22"/>
      <line x1="128" y1="0" x2="128" y2="22"/>
      <line x1="164" y1="0" x2="164" y2="22"/>
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- House shell (being built) -->
  <g transform="translate(160,190)">
    <!-- walls -->
    <rect x="0" y="140" width="192" height="160" rx="10" fill="#f2f2f7" stroke="#cfcfda" stroke-width="4"/>
    <rect x="22" y="200" width="148" height="100" fill="#e9e9f3" stroke="#c1c1cc" stroke-width="3"/>

    <!-- roof -->
    <path d="M-10 146 L96 70 L202 146" fill="#d35f47" stroke="#b34b37" stroke-width="4"/>
    <path d="M30 140 L96 96 L162 140" fill="none" stroke="#b34b37" stroke-width="3" opacity="0.55"/>

    <!-- door / opening -->
    <rect x="72" y="260" width="48" height="40" rx="6" fill="#cfd0d8" stroke="#b0b0bb" stroke-width="3"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Scaffolding/beam hints -->
  <g opacity="0.95">
    <rect x="120" y="130" width="14" height="250" fill="#6d7a8b"/>
    <rect x="378" y="130" width="14" height="250" fill="#6d7a8b"/>
    <g fill="#6d7a8b">
      <rect x="120" y="200" width="272" height="10" rx="3"/>
      <rect x="120" y="260" width="272" height="10" rx="3"/>
      <rect x="120" y="320" width="272" height="10" rx="3"/>
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- Small construction crane hook hint -->
  <g transform="translate(310,55)" opacity="0.95">
    <rect x="0" y="0" width="10" height="170" rx="4" fill="#9aa3af"/>
    <path d="M5 0 L120 0" stroke="#9aa3af" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <path d="M120 0 L92 35" stroke="#9aa3af" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <path d="M92 35 L92 165" stroke="#9aa3af" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <rect x="87" y="170" width="10" height="50" rx="3" fill="#7f8793"/>
    <path d="M82 220 L92 205 L102 220" fill="#7f8793"/>
    <rect x="80" y="225" width="24" height="18" rx="4" fill="#6f7783"/>
  </g>

</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating a build icon**

I'm focused on designing the word "build" using visuals instead of text. I think icons like bricks and a house under construction would work well. I want to create a simple foundation base and stack bricks with alternating colors. A crane could add a nice touch. I’m considering a basic roof outline with a triangle and maybe even scaffolding, making sure to center everything and stick to SVG shapes without any letters. That sounds fun!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}

response.rawBody: {"id":"gen-1773769812-pDvBOofqqAkL2ronoCR5","object":"chat.completion","created":1773769812,"model":"openai/gpt-5.4-nano-20260317","provider":"OpenAI","system_fingerprint":null,"choices":[{"index":0,"logprobs":null,"finish_reason":"stop","native_finish_reason":"completed","message":{"role":"assistant","content":"{ \"guesses\": [\"hydraulicpress\", \"press\", \"piston\", \"ram\", \"hydraulic\", \"cylinder\", \"jack\", \"clamp\", \"vise\", \"stamping\", \"die\", \"pneumatic\", \"lever\", \"wedge\", \"scissor\", \"hoist\", \"lift\", \"machine\", \"pressbrake\", \"forging\"] }","refusal":null,"reasoning":"**Identifying the drawing**\n\nI need to figure out what this drawing is. It seems to resemble a shelving unit or a cabinet with a heavy door. The red and orange frame sets a scene with a central red folded bracket. There’s a hammer, vertical dark grey rods, and diagonal beams. I wonder if it features a lift or catapult mechanism? The red triangular wedge looks like it could be a brake caliper or a vice. Hmm, it might actually represent a bench vise with orange jaws and a screw handle!**Analyzing the drawing**\n\nI see two jaws at the bottom, with a triangular wedge in the center and a vertical rod on the right, which looks like a screw and handle on top. It feels like it could be a punching machine, but the red part also resembles a die. The grey rods make me question if it's a railcar, a door closer, or maybe even a circuit breaker. After considering everything, it seems most likely a hydraulic press with a cross-section showing its structure. I'll focus on making 20 unique one-word guesses.","reasoning_details":[{"type":"reasoning.summary","summary":"**Identifying the drawing**\n\nI need to figure out what this drawing is. It seems to resemble a shelving unit or a cabinet with a heavy door. The red and orange frame sets a scene with a central red folded bracket. There’s a hammer, vertical dark grey rods, and diagonal beams. I wonder if it features a lift or catapult mechanism? The red triangular wedge looks like it could be a brake caliper or a vice. Hmm, it might actually represent a bench vise with orange jaws and a screw handle!**Analyzing the drawing**\n\nI see two jaws at the bottom, with a triangular wedge in the center and a vertical rod on the right, which looks like a screw and handle on top. It feels like it could be a punching machine, but the red part also resembles a die. The grey rods make me question if it's a railcar, a door closer, or maybe even a circuit breaker. message.reasoning:
**Identifying the drawing**

I need to figure out what this drawing is. It seems to resemble a shelving unit or a cabinet with a heavy door. The red and orange frame sets a scene with a central red folded bracket. There’s a hammer, vertical dark grey rods, and diagonal beams. I wonder if it features a lift or catapult mechanism? The red triangular wedge looks like it could be a brake caliper or a vice. Hmm, it might actually represent a bench vise with orange jaws and a screw handle!**Analyzing the drawing**

I see two jaws at the bottom, with a triangular wedge in the center and a vertical rod on the right, which looks like a screw and handle on top. It feels like it could be a punching machine, but the red part also resembles a die. The grey rods make me question if it's a railcar, a door closer, or maybe even a circuit breaker. After considering everything, it seems most likely a hydraulic press with a cross-section showing its structure. I'll focus on making 20 unique one-word guesses.
